qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Backup restore rehydrates attacker-supplied history paths into arbitrary file reads
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is a backup-restore and preview-path bug in `backups/restore.py`, `Watch.history`, and `preview.py`, while the advisory fixes dangerous XPath function execution in `ValidateCSSJSONXPATHInput` and `html_tools.py:xpath_filter()` via `SafeXPath3Parser`.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's XPath `unparsed-text()` root cause. The only real session finding is an arbitrary file read caused by backup restore rehydrating attacker-chosen history paths and preview later opening those paths.
evidence: The advisory describes `include_filters` XPath expressions reaching `changedetectionio/html_tools.py:xpath_filter()` and `elementpath.select(..., parser=XPath3Parser)`, with the fix replacing `XPath3Parser` with `SafeXPath3Parser` in `forms.py` and `html_tools.py`.

The accepted session finding instead uses `POST /backups/restore/start` to import a crafted ZIP, preserves attacker-chosen absolute paths from `history.txt`, and reads them through `GET /preview/<uuid>?version=<timestamp>`.

Both issues can disclose files, but they rely on different entrypoints, different vulnerable files/functions, and different root-cause mechanisms, so the session finding is open-world rather than advisory-matched.
